title: olearia iodochroa
intro: Olearia iodochroa, also known as New Zealand tree daisy, is a flowering plant native to New Zealand, characterized by its white or pale yellow flowers and woody stems. The plant has been traditionally used in herbal medicine for its anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, particularly in treating skin conditions, wounds, and respiratory issues. Olearia iodochroa has been found to have various health benefits, including reducing inflammation, improving wound healing, and exhibiting antimicrobial activity. Herbal preparations of Olearia iodochroa can be found in teas, tinctures, and topical creams, although its use and efficacy may vary depending on the specific formulation and individual needs.
